configurations.name: DD_ACTION_EXECUTION_ID
configurations.description: Sets the action execution ID reported as CI metadata for AWS CodePipeline builds. This value is used as the CI job identifier when running a CodeBuild job initiated by CodePipeline.

configurations.name: DD_AGENT_HOST
configurations.description: The host name to use to connect the Datadog agent for traces. The host name can be IPv4, IPv6, or a domain name. If DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL is specified, the value of DD_AGENT_HOST is ignored.

configurations.name: DD_API_KEY
configurations.description: To use the Datadog CLI in Azure Cloud Shell, open a cloud shell, set your API key and site in the `DD_API_KEY` and `DD_SITE` environment variables, and use `npx` to run the CLI directly:

configurations.name: DD_API_KEY
configurations.description: Sets the API key used to authenticate outbound requests when running in agentless mode (sending data directly instead of via a local agent). This key is required for agentless CI test reporting and LLM Observability agentless mode.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_DOWNSTREAM_REQUEST_BODY_ANALYSIS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Defines the probability of a downstream request body being sampled, or said differently, defines the overall number of requests for which the request and response body should be sampled / analysed.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_DOWNSTREAM_REQUEST_BODY_ANALYSIS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Defines the probability of a downstream request body being sampled, or said differently, defines the overall number of requests for which the request and response body should be sampled / analysed.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether API endpoint definitions are collected for API catalog features. If unset, endpoint collection is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_MESSAGE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of API endpoints included in a single endpoint-collection telemetry message. If unset, a default limit is used.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_MAX_DOWNSTREAM_REQUEST_BODY_ANALYSIS
configurations.description: The maximum number of downstream requests per request for which the request and response body should be analysed.

configurations.name: DD_APM_TRACING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables sending APM trace data. When disabled, tracing still runs to support other products, but only periodic heartbeat traces and manually kept traces are sent.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_BODY_PARSING_SIZE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Maximum size of the bodies to be processed in bytes. If set to `0`, the bodies are not processed. (Recommended value: `10000000` (10MB))

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_BODY_PARSING_SIZE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of bytes of HTTP request and response bodies to buffer and analyze for security features when running behind a proxy/external processing integration. Values of 0 or less disable body analysis, and bodies larger than the limit are truncated.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the AppSec product inside the tracer. When enabled the tracer initializes the AppSec module (in-app WAF, header/body collection and AppSec telemetry)

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_HTML
configurations.description: Path to a local HTML template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be HTML. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_HTML
configurations.description: Path to a local HTML template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be HTML. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_JSON
configurations.description: Path to a local JSON template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be JSON. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_JSON
configurations.description: Path to a local JSON template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be JSON. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACE_DEPTH
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of stack frames retained for any captured stacktrace attached to AppSec events. Tracers should prioritise top-of-stack frames and truncate deeper frames when this limit is reached. The setting helps keep evidentiary stacktraces compact while preserving the most actionable frames

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_OBFUSCATION_PARAMETER_KEY_REGEXP
configurations.description: A regular expression used by the AppSec obfuscator to identify parameter keys that must cause full redaction. When any `key_path` string matches this regexp the entire parameter is replaced by `<redacted by datadog>`. The default key regexp matches common secret/key names (password, token, authorization, jwt, etc.) an empty string disables the key regexp

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_OBFUSCATION_PARAMETER_VALUE_REGEXP
configurations.description: Regular expression used to find and redact sensitive substrings inside parameter values and highlights. Matches are replaced with `<redacted by datadog>`. This regexp complements the key regexp: the key regexp takes precedence; when no key match exists, the value regexp runs. The default pattern targeted at tokens, JWTs, private key blocks, and other credential shapes. Empty string disables the value regexp

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RULES
configurations.description: Override the default rules file provided. Must be a path to a valid JSON rules file

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RULES
configurations.description: Override the default rules file provided. Must be a path to a valid JSON rules file

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_SCA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the tracer's runtime Software Composition Analysis (Runtime SCA) capability, which reports runtime dependency/SBOM information and powers runtime vulnerability detection. Useful for testing and demonstrations as well as production runtime vulnerability workflows; SCA may have billing/usage implications and language compatibility constraints described in the SCA runbooks

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_SCA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the tracer's runtime Software Composition Analysis (Runtime SCA) capability, which reports runtime dependency/SBOM information and powers runtime vulnerability detection. Useful for testing and demonstrations as well as production runtime vulnerability workflows; SCA may have billing/usage implications and language compatibility constraints described in the SCA runbooks

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_STACK_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables security-related stack traces generation when security events occur.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_WAF_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Limits the WAF synchronous execution time (in microseconds).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AGENTLESS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Agentless mode in CI Visibility. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AGENTLESS_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the intake base URL used for agentless CI test reporting. When set, payloads are sent to this URL instead of the default site-based intake URL.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AUTO_INSTRUMENTATION_PROVIDER
configurations.description: Marks CI test sessions as running under an auto-instrumentation workflow. Any non-empty value enables this marker for telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enabling or disabling CI Visibility

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_FLAKY_RETRY_COUNT
configurations.description: Can be set to any non-negative number to change the maximum number of retries per test case.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_FLAKY_RETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: A kill switch for the auto test retries feature.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_IMPACTED_TESTS_DETECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility impacted tests detection (TIA). When enabled, impacted-tests detection is requested/applied as part of execution settings propagated to child processes.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_INTERNAL_PARALLEL_EARLY_FLAKE_DETECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether early flake detection retries can run in parallel in CI test reporting. Defaults to false.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_LOGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Logs direct submission. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_SUBTEST_FEATURES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Kill-switch for subtest-specific CI test management and retry features. Set to false to disable subtest features even if they are enabled by remote settings.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TOTAL_FLAKY_RETRY_COUNT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum total number of automatic test retries allowed across the entire test session. This limit is applied when automatic flaky-test retries are enabled and defaults to 1000.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_USE_NOOP_TRACER
configurations.description: When enabled in CI test reporting mode, uses a no-op tracer implementation to reduce changes to test behavior. Defaults to false.

configurations.name: DD_CUSTOM_TRACE_ID
configurations.description: Custom variable set by the Jenkins Datadog Plugin for trace IDs.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_STREAMS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Data Streams Monitoring (DSM) and propagation. When enabled, the tracer registers the DSM propagator and reports pathway stats/payloads. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_DBM_PROPAGATION_MODE
configurations.description: Enables linking between data sent from APM and the Database Monitoring product when set to `service` or `full`. The `service` option enables the connection between DBM and APM services. The `full` option enables connection between database spans with database query events. Available for Postgres and MySQL.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_HOST
configurations.description: Override the address of the trace Agent host that the default tracer attempts to submit DogStatsD metrics to. Use `DD_AGENT_HOST` to override `DD_DOGSTATSD_HOST`.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT
configurations.description: The port of the DogStatsD Agent that metrics are submitted to. If the Agent configuration sets `dogstatsd_port` or `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` to something other than the default `8125`, then this tracing library `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` must match it.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Dynamic Instrumentation.

configurations.name: DD_ENV
configurations.description: Sets the global `env` for all data emitted by the Agent. If `env` is not present in your trace data, this variable is used.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_FLAGGING_PROVIDER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the experimental feature-flag provider that evaluates flags from remote configuration. If not enabled, the provider is a no-op and returns default values.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_PROPAGATE_PROCESS_TAGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables experimental propagation of process tags in payloads such as spans and telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_PROPAGATE_PROCESS_TAGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether process-level tags (such as entrypoint metadata) are collected and included in tracing metadata. If unset, this is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_EXTERNAL_ENV
configurations.description: Sets an external environment identifier attached to emitted telemetry for origin and environment attribution.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_BRANCH
configurations.description: Overrides the Git branch value used by CI Visibility for repository and commit correlation.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_DATE
configurations.description: The date when the author submitted the commit expressed in ISO 8601 format.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit author email reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_NAME
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit author name reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_DATE
configurations.description: The date when the committer submitted the commit expressed in ISO 8601 format.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit committer email reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_NAME
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit committer name reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_MESSAGE
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit message reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_SHA
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit SHA reported as CI metadata and used for git metadata tagging when enabled.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_REPOSITORY_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the git repository URL reported as CI metadata and used for git metadata tagging when enabled. Any embedded credentials in the URL are stripped before use.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_TAG
configurations.description: Overrides the Git tag used by CI Visibility for repository and release correlation.

configurations.name: DD_HAPROXY_SPOA_HEALTHCHECK_PORT
configurations.description: Configure the port used for healthcheck server for the HAProxy SPOA integration

configurations.name: DD_HAPROXY_SPOA_HOST
configurations.description: Configure the host used for the HAProxy SPOA integration

configurations.name: DD_HAPROXY_SPOA_PORT
configurations.description: Configure the port used for the HAProxy SPOA integration

configurations.name: DD_HOSTNAME
configurations.description: Overrides the hostname used for tagging and telemetry when hostname auto-detection is unreliable. The value must be a valid hostname; invalid values are ignored and hostname auto-detection is used instead.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_ID
configurations.description: Sets the install ID included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers. This can be used to correlate telemetry to a specific installation.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_TIME
configurations.description: Sets the install time included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_TYPE
configurations.description: Sets the install type included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_TELEMETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Datadog may collect environmental and diagnostic information about your system to improve the product. When false, this telemetry data will not be collected. **Default**: true

configurations.name: DD_KEY
configurations.description: Internal placeholder configuration key used for configuration-source tests and examples. It is not used by the tracer at runtime.

configurations.name: DD_LLMOBS_AGENTLESS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether LLM Observability sends data directly to the intake (agentless mode) or through an agent/proxy. If unset, the mode is chosen automatically based on agent support; setting it to false requires an agent that supports LLM Observability.

configurations.name: DD_LLMOBS_ML_APP
configurations.description: Sets the default ML app name used for LLM Observability data. This value is required when LLM Observability is enabled.

configurations.name: DD_LOGGING_RATE
configurations.description: Sets how often aggregated internal errors are flushed to logs, in seconds. Set to 0 to log errors immediately.

configurations.name: DD_METRICS_OTEL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables OpenTelemetry metrics export. Metrics are disabled by default and are only enabled when this is set to a truthy value.

configurations.name: DD_PIPELINE_EXECUTION_ID
configurations.description: Sets the pipeline execution ID reported as CI metadata for AWS CodePipeline builds. This value is used as the CI pipeline identifier when running a CodeBuild job initiated by CodePipeline.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_AGENTLESS
configurations.description: Enables agentless profile upload, sending profiles directly to the profiling intake instead of through a local agent.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_CODE_HOTSPOTS_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether spans add pprof labels used to link profiles to trace code hotspots (span id and local root span id). Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_DEBUG_COMPRESSION_SETTINGS
configurations.description: Controls the compression algorithm and level used when preparing profiles for upload (for example, legacy, gzip, or zstd-<level>). Defaults to zstd.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_DELTA
configurations.description: Controls whether delta profiles are enabled. When enabled, profile values are reported as changes since the previous profile instead of absolute values.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable the profiler by setting `-Ddd.profiling.enabled` flag or `DD_PROFILING_ENABLED` environment variable to `true`.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ENABLED
configurations.description: **Configuration**: profiling **Default**: false Whether to enable profiling.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether spans add a pprof label with the top-level request endpoint so profiles can be grouped by endpoint. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ENDPOINT_COUNT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables counting endpoint hits while the profiler is running and includes those counts with uploaded profiling data. Defaults to false.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXECUTION_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether runtime execution traces are collected and uploaded. Defaults to enabled on amd64 and arm64.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXECUTION_TRACE_LIMIT_BYTES
configurations.description: Sets the approximate maximum size, in bytes, of a collected execution trace. Defaults to 5MB.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXECUTION_TRACE_PERIOD
configurations.description: Sets the time between execution trace collections. Defaults to 15m.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_FLUSH_ON_EXIT
configurations.description: Controls whether the profiler attempts to finish uploading buffered profiles when the process is stopping. When disabled, pending uploads are cancelled on shutdown.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_OUTPUT_DIR
configurations.description: Writes a copy of collected profiles to the specified directory for debugging. No cleanup is performed, so the directory can grow over time.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Controls timeout behavior for tracer operations when communicating with external components.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Sets the timeout for profile upload HTTP requests. Defaults to 10s and must be greater than 0.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_URL
configurations.description: Profiling: explicit profiling intake URL. If set, the tracer uses this URL regardless of agentless/agent-based profiling settings; otherwise it targets the agentless intake (`https://intake.profile.<site>/api/v2/profile`) when agentless, or the Datadog Agent endpoint (`<agentUrl>/profiling/v1/input`) when not agentless.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the profiling intake URL used for agentless uploads. This is primarily intended for testing or debugging.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_WAIT_PROFILE
configurations.description: Enables an experimental goroutine wait profile when set. This collects stack traces and wait durations for goroutines that remain blocked for more than a minute.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_WAIT_PROFILE_MAX_GOROUTINES
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of goroutines allowed when collecting the goroutine wait profile. If the process exceeds this limit, the wait profile is skipped.

configurations.name: DD_RC_TUF_ROOT
configurations.description: Internal remote-configuration setting for specifying a TUF root metadata value. This setting is currently not used and has no effect.

configurations.name: DD_REMOTE_CONFIGURATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable or disable remote configuration. Also accepts the alias `DD_REMOTE_CONFIG_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_REMOTE_CONFIG_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS
configurations.description: **Since**: 0.2.0 Sets how often, in seconds, the Datadog Agent is queried for Remote Configuration updates.

configurations.name: DD_REQUEST_MIRROR_HEALTHCHECK_ADDR
configurations.description: Sets the address (host:port) for the request-mirror health check HTTP server to listen on. Defaults to :8081.

configurations.name: DD_REQUEST_MIRROR_LISTEN_ADDR
configurations.description: Sets the address (host:port) for the request-mirror HTTP server to listen on. Defaults to :8080.

configurations.name: DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the collection of runtime metrics (such as garbage collection stats, memory usage, and thread counts) for the application.

configurations.name: DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_V2_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether runtime metrics v2 are emitted. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE
configurations.description: Sets the default service name used for most spans. SDKs may set a different service name for inferred services. Integration spans may use their own default names, which can differ from the value specified in DD_SERVICE

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE
configurations.description: Sets the service name used for telemetry and generated spans. If unset, the service name defaults to the process name.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE_EXTENSION_HEALTHCHECK_PORT
configurations.description: Sets the port for the service-extension health check HTTP server to listen on. Defaults to 80.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE_EXTENSION_HOST
configurations.description: Sets the IP address the service extension servers listen on. Defaults to 0.0.0.0.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE_EXTENSION_OBSERVABILITY_MODE
configurations.description: Enables observability mode for the service extension. When enabled, blocking actions are disabled.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE_EXTENSION_PORT
configurations.description: Sets the port for the service extension gRPC server to listen on. Defaults to 443.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE_EXTENSION_TLS
configurations.description: Controls whether TLS is enabled for the service extension gRPC server. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE_EXTENSION_TLS_CERT_FILE
configurations.description: Change the default gRPC TLS layer cert.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE_EXTENSION_TLS_KEY_FILE
configurations.description: Change the default gRPC TLS layer key.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE_MAPPING
configurations.description: **Example**: `mysql:my-mysql-service-name-db,pg:my-pg-service-name-db` Provide service names for each plugin. Accepts comma separated `plugin:service-name` pairs, with or without spaces.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE_MAPPING
configurations.description: Define service name mappings to allow renaming services in traces, for example: `postgres:postgresql,defaultdb:postgresql`. Available in version 0.47+.

configurations.name: DD_SITE
configurations.description: If you're not using the Datadog US1 site, set your Datadog site with a `DD_SITE` environment variable under the configuration tab of your function app, or copy the site parameter into the function code on line 21.

configurations.name: DD_SITE
configurations.description: Sets the site domain used to build agentless intake URLs. If unset, a default site is used.

configurations.name: DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES
configurations.description: Span sampling rules to keep individual spans when the rest of the trace would otherwise be dropped. A JSON array of objects. Rules are applied in configured order to determine the span's sample rate. The sample_rate value must be between 0.0 and 1.0 (inclusive). For more information, see Ingestion Mechanisms. Example: - Set the span sample rate to 50% for the service my-service and operation name http.request, up to 50 traces per second: '[{"service": "my-service", "name": "http.request", "sample_rate":0.5, "max_per_second": 50}]'

configurations.name: DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES_FILE
configurations.description: Points to a JSON file that contains the span sampling rules. DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES takes precedence over this variable. See DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES for the rule format.

configurations.name: DD_TAGS
configurations.description: Custom tags applied to traces profiles and metrics, provided as comma-separated key:value pairs (e.g. `layer:api,team:intake`).

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_DEBUG
configurations.description: Enables debug mode for instrumentation telemetry. When set, it forces telemetry debug mode on regardless of other configuration.

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_DEPENDENCY_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Telemetry: enables the dependency collection service that detects application dependencies via classloading and reports them. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
configurations.description: : Interval in seconds for sending telemetry heartbeat messages.

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Sets the interval for the telemetry status heartbeat (in seconds)

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_LOG_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: **Default**: true : Enable or disable log collection for telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_METRICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether instrumentation telemetry sends internal metrics. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_TEST_AGENT_HOST
configurations.description: Sets the host used by internal instrumentation validation tests to reach a test agent. This setting is not used for runtime tracing.

configurations.name: DD_TEST_AGENT_PORT
configurations.description: Sets the port used by internal instrumentation validation tests to reach a test agent. This setting is not used for runtime tracing.

configurations.name: DD_TEST_MANAGEMENT_ATTEMPT_TO_FIX_RETRIES
configurations.description: CI Visibility Test Management: overrides the number of retry executions for tests marked as "attempt to fix". If unset, the value from backend/remote settings is used.

configurations.name: DD_TEST_MANAGEMENT_ATTEMPT_TO_FIX_RETRIES
configurations.description: Overrides the maximum number of retries for the test management attempt-to-fix feature. When set, it replaces the value provided by remote settings.

configurations.name: DD_TEST_MANAGEMENT_ENABLED
configurations.description: CI Visibility Test Management: enables Test Management features (disabled/quarantined/attempt-to-fix tests) when backend/remote settings allow. Acts as a local kill-switch. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TEST_OPTIMIZATION_ENV_DATA_FILE
configurations.description: Sets the path to a JSON file containing CI/environment metadata used for test reporting. If not set, a default file next to the test binary is used.

configurations.name: DD_TEST_SESSION_NAME
configurations.description: If `DD_TEST_SESSION_NAME` is not specified, the default value used is a combination of the:

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_128_BIT_TRACEID_GENERATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether new traces use 128-bit W3C trace IDs (32-character hexadecimal strings) or 64-bit Datadog trace IDs (16-character hexadecimal strings). The default is true to support W3C trace context propagation.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_128_BIT_TRACEID_LOGGING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether 128-bit trace IDs are logged in their full 32-character format or truncated to 16 characters. Set to false for compatibility with systems that expect the shorter format.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ABANDONED_SPAN_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Sets the duration after which an unfinished span is considered abandoned for debugging. Defaults to 10m.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT
configurations.description: The port of the Trace Agent that the tracer submits to. If the Agent configuration sets receiver_port or DD_APM_RECEIVER_PORT to something other than the default 8126, then DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT or DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL must match it.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL
configurations.description: Sets the URL endpoint where profiles are sent. Overrides `DD_AGENT_HOST` and `DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT` if set. Defaults to `http://<DD_AGENT_HOST>:<DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT>`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL
configurations.description: The URL for connecting the tracer to the Datadog agent. Valid URL schemas include http:// and unix:// (UNIX Domain Sockets). This value takes precedence over DD_AGENT_HOST and DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT if set.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the legacy tracing analytics feature (deprecated).

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Trace Analytics by default. When enabled for an integration, instrumentations set the `analytics.sample_rate` metric on spans (using the configured per-integration sample rate). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AWS_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the AWS SDK integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_BUNTDB_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the BuntDB integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CHI_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the chi integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CLIENT_HOSTNAME_COMPAT
configurations.description: Internal compatibility toggle for legacy client-hostname behavior. This setting is currently not used and has no effect.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the automatic collection of client IP addresses from HTTP request headers. When enabled, the IP address is stored in the http.client_ip span tag.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_HEADER
configurations.description: Configures a custom header name from which to source the http.client_ip tag value. If this variable is set, all other IP-related headers are ignored (for example, setting DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_HEADER=custom-ip-header and including the header custom-ip-header: 5.6.7.9 in a request results in a span tagged with "http.client_ip": "5.6.7.9"). If an empty string or null value is passed, IP headers are queried in this order: - x-forwarded-for - x-real-ip - true-client-ip - x-client-ip - x-forwarded - forwarded-for - x-cluster-client-ip - fastly-client-ip - cf-connecting-ip - cf-connecting-ipv6

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CONSUL_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Consul integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DEBUG
configurations.description: **Note:** For versions below 2.X, debug mode could be enabled programmatically inside the tracer initialization but this is no longer supported.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DEBUG
configurations.description: Enable debug logging in the tracer. Use OTEL_LOG_LEVEL as an alias

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DEBUG_ABANDONED_SPANS
configurations.description: The Datadog Go Tracer also supports logging for potentially abandoned spans. To enable this debug mode in Go, set the environment variable DD_TRACE_DEBUG_ABANDONED_SPANS=true. To change the duration after which spans are considered abandoned (default=10m), set the environment variable DD_TRACE_ABANDONED_SPAN_TIMEOUT to the desired time duration. Abandoned span logs appear at the Info level.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DEBUG_SEELOG_WORKAROUND
configurations.description: Controls whether a workaround for seelog-related goroutine leaks is applied. Set to false to disable the workaround.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DEBUG_STACK
configurations.description: Controls whether stack traces are captured for spans that finish with an error. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ELASTIC_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Elasticsearch integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: **Default**: true Enable web framework and library instrumentation. When false, the application code doesn't generate any traces. See also DD_APM_TRACING_ENABLED.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_FEATURES
configurations.description: Comma- or space-separated list of tracer feature flags. Use this to enable optional or experimental behavior such as "discovery".

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_FIBER_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Fiber integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GIN_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Gin integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GIT_METADATA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Git metadata: when enabled, adds git repository URL and commit SHA tags to the root span (used for linking traces to source code). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GOCQL_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the gocql integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GOCQL_COMPAT
configurations.description: Sets a compatibility version for the gocql integration. When set to a valid semver value (for example, v1.65.0), it adjusts how certain cluster tags are reported for older versions.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GOOGLE_API_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Google API integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GOPG_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the go-pg integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GQLGEN_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the gqlgen integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GRAPHQL_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables analytics for the GraphQL integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GRAPHQL_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by GraphQL integrations by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GRAPHQL_ERROR_EXTENSIONS
configurations.description: Specifies which GraphQL error extensions to capture. A comma-separated list of extension keys to capture. Empty or not present means no extensions are captured.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GRPC_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the gRPC integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HEADER_TAGS
configurations.description: Automatically apply specified HTTP headers as span tags. If a custom tag name is not specified, the tag key defaults to http.request.headers. for request headers and http.response.headers. for response headers.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTPROUTER_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the httprouter integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the net/http integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_CLIENT_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of HTTP status codes and ranges to treat as errors for HTTP client spans (for example, 400-499,500). When set, matching responses mark the span as an error.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_CLIENT_TAG_QUERY_STRING
configurations.description: By default, query string parameters and fragments are added to the `http.url` tag on web client spans. Set to `false` to prevent the collection of this data.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_SERVER_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: A range of errors can be accepted. By default 5xx status codes are reported as errors for http servers. This configuration overrides that. Ex. `dd.trace.http.server.error.statuses=500,502-599`

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_SERVER_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of HTTP status codes and ranges to treat as errors for HTTP server spans (for example, 500-599,404). When unset, 5xx responses are treated as errors by default.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_URL_QUERY_STRING_DISABLED
configurations.description: Disables including URL query strings in the http.url tag for incoming HTTP requests. When disabled, only the path is reported.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_INFERRED_PROXY_SERVICES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Inferred proxy services: when enabled, registers the inferred-proxy propagator to extract `x-dd-proxy*` headers and start an inferred proxy span (currently supports `x-dd-proxy=aws-apigateway`) as a parent of the server span, using header values for service/resource/tags. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_KAFKA_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by Kafka integrations by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_LEVELDB_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the LevelDB integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_LOG_DIRECTORY
configurations.description: Sets a directory where tracer logs are written. The directory must exist; otherwise logs remain on stderr.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_MCP_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: This setting is currently not used by the MCP integration and has no effect.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_MEMCACHE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Memcache integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_MGO_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the mgo integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_MONGO_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by MongoDB integrations by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_MUX_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the gorilla/mux integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_NEGRONI_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Negroni integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_OBFUSCATION_QUERY_STRING_REGEXP
configurations.description: Applies a regex to redact sensitive data from query strings on incoming HTTP requests. The default regex matches various sensitive data patterns, including passwords, tokens, API keys, private keys, and authorization terms. Matches are replaced with . If an empty string is passed, no obfuscation occurs. The resulting value is reported in the http.url tag.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_OBFUSCATION_QUERY_STRING_REGEXP
configurations.description: Applies a regex to redact sensitive data from query strings on incoming HTTP requests. The default regex matches various sensitive data patterns, including passwords, tokens, API keys, private keys, and authorization terms. Matches are replaced with <redacted>. If an empty string is passed, no obfuscation occurs. The resulting value is reported in the http.url tag.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PARTIAL_FLUSH_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables incrementally flushing large traces to the Datadog Agent, reducing the chance of rejection by the Agent. Use only when you have long-lived traces or traces with many spans.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PARTIAL_FLUSH_MIN_SPANS
configurations.description: Minimum number of spans in a trace before partial flush is triggered.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PARTIAL_FLUSH_MIN_SPANS
configurations.description: Sets the minimum number of finished spans required to trigger a partial flush when partial flushing is enabled. Defaults to 1000; invalid values are ignored.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PEER_SERVICE_DEFAULTS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Peer service defaults (Naming Schema v0): when enabled, computes default `peer.service` values for eligible spans using the v1 peer-service default algorithm; when disabled (default), Naming Schema v0 does not compute peer.service defaults.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PEER_SERVICE_MAPPING
configurations.description: Maps detected peer service names to normalized values before they are attached to spans.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_EXTRACT_FIRST
configurations.description: When set to true, stops extracting after the first successful trace context extraction.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of header formats from which to attempt to inject and extract distributed tracing propagation data. The first format found with complete and valid headers is used to define the trace to continue. The more specific DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_INJECT and DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_EXTRACT configurations take priority when present.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of header formats from which to attempt to inject and extract distributed tracing propagation data. The first format found with complete and valid headers is used to define the trace to continue. The more specific DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_INJECT and DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_EXTRACT configurations take priority when present.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_EXTRACT
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of header formats from which to attempt to extract distributed tracing propagation data. The first format found with complete and valid headers is used to define the trace to continue.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_EXTRACT
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of header formats from which to attempt to extract distributed tracing propagation data. The first format found with complete and valid headers is used to define the trace to continue.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_INJECT
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of header formats to include to propagate distributed traces between services.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_INJECT
configurations.description: comma-separated list of header formats to include to propagate distributed traces between services.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RATE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of traces to sample per second; applies only when either DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES or DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE is set.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REDIGO_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Redigo integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REDIS_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by Redis integrations by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REDIS_RAW_COMMAND
configurations.description: Controls whether Redis client spans include a tag with the raw command. This can expose sensitive data, so it is disabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REMOVE_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_NAMES_ENABLED
configurations.description: When enabled, disables inferred-services/integration service naming by making Naming Schema v0 not allow inferred services (so integrations that rely on inferred services fall back to application service naming)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REPORT_HOSTNAME
configurations.description: Whether to report the system's hostname for each trace. When disabled, the hostname of the Agent is used instead.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESOURCE_RENAMING_ALWAYS_SIMPLIFIED_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Always set the http.endpoint tag with the results of the simplification algorithm of Resource Renaming

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESOURCE_RENAMING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether the http.endpoint tag is computed and added for incoming HTTP requests. This is disabled by default unless application security is enabled at startup, and can be explicitly enabled or disabled with this setting.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESTFUL_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the go-restful integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RETRY_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Sets the interval for retrying submitting payloads to the agent

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Controls the ingestion sample rate (between 0.0 and 1.0) between the Agent and the backend.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Controls the ingestion sample rate (between 0.0 and 1.0) between the Agent and the backend.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES
configurations.description: Configures custom sampling rules for traces. Rules are evaluated in order, and the first matching rule determines the sampling rate. If no rules match, the default sampling rate is used. For more information about how these configurations affect trace ingestion, see Ingestion Mechanisms.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES
configurations.description: JSON array of sampling rules. The rule is matched in order of specification. The first match in a list is used. Per entry: The item "sample_rate" is required in decimal format. The item "service" is optional in regular expression format, to match on service name. The item "name" is optional in regular expression format, to match on operation name. To give a rate of 50% to any traces in a service starting with the text "cart": '[{"sample_rate":0.5, "service":"cart.*"}]' To give a rate of 20% to any traces which have an operation name of "http.request": '[{"sample_rate":0.2, "name":"http.request"}]' To give a rate of 100% to any traces within a service named "background" and with an operation name of "sql.query": '[{"sample_rate":1.0, "service":"background", "name":"sql.query"}] To give a rate of 10% to all traces '[{"sample_rate":0.1}]' To configure multiple rules, separate by semi-colon and order from most specific to least specific: '[{"sample_rate":0.5, "service":"cart.*"}, {"sample_rate":0.2, "name":"http.request"}, {"sample_rate":1.0, "service":"background", "name":"sql.query"}, {"sample_rate":0.1}]' If no rules are specified, or none match, default internal sampling logic will be used.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES_FILE
configurations.description: Path to a file containing a JSON array of trace sampling rules. This is used when DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES is not set.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SARAMA_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Sarama integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SOURCE_HOSTNAME
configurations.description: Sets the hostname reported with tracing data. When set, hostname reporting is enabled and uses this value instead of auto-detection.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPAN_ATTRIBUTE_SCHEMA
configurations.description: Selects the span attribute and naming schema version used by integrations. Supported values are v0 (default) and v1; invalid values fall back to v0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPAN_ATTRIBUTE_SCHEMA
configurations.description: Sets the schema version for service naming and span attributes. Accepted values are: "v1", "v0". Default value is "v0".

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SQL_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the database/sql integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SQL_COMMENT_INJECTION_MODE
configurations.description: Controls SQL comment injection used for database monitoring correlation. Supported values are disabled, service, and full.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STARTUP_LOGS
configurations.description: Enable startup configuration and diagnostic log.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STARTUP_LOGS
configurations.description: Enable startup configuration and diagnostic log.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STATS_COMPUTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace-stats computation in the tracer for stats payload generation and ingestion-side metrics.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STATS_COMPUTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether client-side trace statistics computation is enabled. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TWIRP_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Twirp integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_V1_PAYLOAD_FORMAT_ENABLED
configurations.description: When enabled and the agent supports it, sends traces using the v1 payload format. Defaults to false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VALKEY_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: This setting is currently not used by the valkey-go integration and has no effect.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VALKEY_RAW_COMMAND
configurations.description: Controls whether Valkey client spans include a tag with the raw command. This can expose sensitive data, so it is disabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VAULT_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Vault integration by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_X_DATADOG_TAGS_MAX_LENGTH
configurations.description: Maximum length of the `x-datadog-tags` header for trace tag propagation. Set to 0 to disable.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE__ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for integrations that use the default (unprefixed) analytics toggle by setting the analytics sample rate to 1.0.

configurations.name: DD_VERSION
configurations.description: Set the application's version, for example: `1.2.3` or `6c44da20`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Specifies the base URL for sending OTLP data for all signals unless overridden. **Default (gRPC)**: `http://localhost:4317`. **Default (HTTP)**: `http://localhost:4318`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Sets the OTLP exporter endpoint used by OpenTelemetry metrics export. When set, it overrides the default endpoint derived from agent configuration.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS
configurations.description: Configuration key to set custom headers for OTLP export (fallback for metrics-specific headers). Used when metrics-specific OTLP headers are not set. Format: api-key=key,other=value.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: If non-empty, used as the OTLP metrics endpoint. Otherwise falls back to `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`, then to `http://<DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL host>:4317/4318` based on protocol. For `http/protobuf`, `/v1/metrics` is appended if missing.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the URL for sending OTLP metrics data. Takes precedence over the general OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT. **Default (gRPC)**: http://localhost:4317. **Default (HTTP)**: http://localhost:4318/v1/metrics. **Notes**: For HTTP protocols, the SDK will automatically append /v1/metrics if the general OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is used as a fallback.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_HEADERS
configurations.description: Specifies a comma-separated list of key-value pairs to be used as headers on outgoing OTLP metrics requests (for example, `api-key=key,other-config=value`). Takes precedence over the general `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_HEADERS
configurations.description: Sets additional headers to include in OTLP exporter requests for metrics. When set, it takes precedence over OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS for metrics export.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Specifies the OTLP transport protocol to use for metrics data. Takes precedence over the general `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL`. **Accepted values**: `grpc`, `http/protobuf`, `http/json`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Selects the protocol used for OpenTelemetry OTLP metrics export. Only http/protobuf is supported.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TEMPORALITY_PREFERENCE
configurations.description: Controls metrics aggregation temporality. Accepted values: `delta` or `cumulative` (case-insensitive); empty/invalid values are treated as `delta`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Used as the exporter network timeout (ms) for OTLP metrics requests. If set to `0`, the library falls back to `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the timeout (in milliseconds) for a single outgoing OTLP metrics request. Takes precedence over the general OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT. **Default**: 10000 (10s).

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Specifies the transport protocol to use for all signals unless overridden. **Accepted values**: `grpc`, `http/protobuf`, `http/json`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the request timeout for OTLP export (fallback for metrics-specific timeout). Used when the metrics-specific OTLP timeout is not set. Default value is 10000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Controls the OpenTelemetry logs exporter selection. This setting is not supported and is ignored.

configurations.name: OTEL_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: Enables debug mode, producing verbose tracer logs (not recommended for production or sensitive environments) when set to debug. Alias for DD_TRACE_DEBUG.

configurations.name: OTEL_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: OTEL_LOG_LEVEL maps to DD_TRACE_DEBUG and enables the debug mode of the tracer. It only accepts "debug" as a valid value.

configurations.name: OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Specifies the metrics exporter to be used. **Notes**: The only accepted values are `otlp` and `none`. A value of `none` disables the emission of OTel metrics, as well as APM runtime metrics (equivalent to `DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_ENABLED=false`)

configurations.name: OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER
configurations.description: OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER is not used to choose an exporter. It is effectively only honored for none, which forces metrics off. When unset it does not enable metrics on its own it needs DD_METRICS_OTEL_ENABLED set to true to effectively produce metrics

configurations.name: OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_INTERVAL
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the time interval (in milliseconds) between metric export attempts. **Default**: 10000 (10s) **Notes**: This default value is Datadog's recommended configuration and differs from the OpenTelemetry specification's default of 60000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the maximum allowed time (in milliseconds) to collect and export metrics. **Default**: 7500 (7.5s) **Notes**: This default value is Datadog's recommended configuration and differs from the OpenTelemetry specification's default of 30000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_PROPAGATORS
configurations.description: Configures which propagators are used for extracting and injecting trace context.

configurations.name: OTEL_PROPAGATORS
configurations.description: Specifies trace context propagation formats for both extraction and injection (comma-separated list). Lowest precedence; ignored if any other Datadog trace context propagation environment variable is set.

configurations.name: OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES
configurations.description: Set OpenTelemetry resource attributes as a list of `(key, value)` pairs.

configurations.name: OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES
configurations.description: Sets OpenTelemetry resource attributes that are mapped to tracer tags when the corresponding tracer tag setting is not set. Reserved attributes for service, environment, and version are mapped to standard tags, and only the first 10 attributes are applied.

configurations.name: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Your application's default service name, set as the service tag on traces. Alias for DD_SERVICE.

configurations.name: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the application's default service name.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Enables or disables tracing. When disabled, instrumentation still runs but no trace data is sent. Alias for DD_TRACE_ENABLED.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER
configurations.description: OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER is not used to select an exporter. It's only recognized for none, which is treated as an instruction to disable tracing (it maps to DD_TRACE_ENABLED=false). Any other value is considered unsupported and effectively ignored. - If DD_TRACE_ENABLED is set, it takes precedence over OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER. - To enable tracing, ensure DD_TRACE_ENABLED is not set to false and do not set OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER=none (unset it if present, or explicitly set DD_TRACE_ENABLED=true).

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
configurations.description: Sets the trace sampling rate, controlling the fraction of traces kept. Alias for DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
configurations.description: Sets the trace sampler via OpenTelemetry configuration and maps it to the tracer's sample rate when the corresponding setting is not set. Parent-based samplers are supported; unsupported values are ignored.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG
configurations.description: Sets the argument for OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER when using the traceidratio sampler (for example, OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG=0.25). This is used to derive the tracer sample rate when mapping OpenTelemetry configuration.
